summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a 1994 show highlighted by an epic Scarlet>Fire, though the second‑set song choices drag it down.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear, well‑balanced mix – many reviewers call it phenomenal and one of the best Charlie Miller uploads. Musically the concert shines on the first set and the 32‑minute Scarlet>Fire opener, with standout versions of Box of Rain, Ramble on Rose, Fire on the Mountain and a solid Jack Straw start, but the second‑set selection (Corrina, Samba, Looks Like Rain) leaves several fans disappointed.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently rated excellent while show quality is mixed – strong first set and Scarlet>Fire versus weaker second‑set choices, yielding an overall rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear, well‑balanced mix; many reviewers describe the sound as phenomenal and superbly remastered by Charlie Miller.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is energetic and well‑played; the 32‑minute Scarlet>Fire opener electrifies the crowd, but the second set loses momentum with less popular selections like Corrina and Samba, leading to a mixed overall pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work on Scarlet>Fire and other jams is praised as fiery and inspiring, though several reviewers note his vocals sound weak and strained at this point in ’94.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass shines on the Box of Rain and he delivers solid support throughout; one reviewer calls his playing “nice”.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drumming is highlighted as a strong element – one reviewer simply says “check out drums”, indicating an especially tight rhythm section.
